content.problemStatement: You are given a positive integer NNN. Your task is to find the last digit of NNN, i.e., the remainder when NNN is divided by 10.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the last digit of N (i.e., N mod 10).

sampleTestCases.explanation: The last digit of 70 is 0, which is the remainder when 70 is divided by 10.
The last digit of 55 is 5, which is the remainder when 55 is divided by 10.
The last digit of 123 is 3, which is the remainder when 123 is divided by 10.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need the remainder of N when divided by 10. The remainder can be obtained directly using the modulo operator (%). No preprocessing or complex data structures are required.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in O(1) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.
